task_updates.executor_notes: Prepared reviewer-facing execution notes while the migration details are still fresh. Provider-pathway classification to carry into the PR: keep provider-local `DataProviderWrapper`; keep provider-local effect bootstrap (`useEffects`, `useEffectResources`, `useEffectRegistry`); keep/extract `AgentChatBridgeRegistration` as a sibling bridge component that reads store selectors but does not move bridge registration state into the store; keep/extract pending-add plus `ADD_GENERATION_QUERY_PARAM` drain as provider-local/sibling side-effect orchestration; keep provider-local lightbox shell state (`lightboxAssetKey`, `lightboxClipId`, `lightboxQuery`, `lightboxFallbackMedia`) and `onDoubleClickAsset`; keep `navResult` in its existing sibling hook; migrate only the former timeline context assembly surface into `timelineStore.ts`. Ref classification to call out in the PR: kept `editorRef.current` in `VideoEditorProvider`, `dataRef` in `TimelineCanvas` resize flow, `dataRef`/`selectedClipIdsRef`/`additiveSelectionRef`/`interactionStateRef` in `useClipDrag`, `dataRef` plus interaction refs in `TimelineEditor`, `dataRef.current` plus selection coupling in `VideoEditorShell`, and `dataRef`/`pendingOpsRef`/`interactionStateRef` in persistence and poll hooks because those refs still protect pointer-sequence or async freshness; removed the old `useTimelineState.contexts.ts` packaging layer because that part existed primarily to stabilize context identity rather than to guard live gesture state. Mounted-boundary note for the PR: the nullable-safe timeline context hooks were replaced by a provider-scoped Zustand availability boundary, not by a global always-present store. `timelineStore.ts` exposes safe selector hooks that return `null` when no `TimelineStoreProvider` is mounted, preserving the `useAddToVideoEditor` staged-vs-mounted contract. Telemetry caveat for reviewers: the existing M0 `useRenderBudget` instrumentation aggregates counts over an interaction window, so scroll-gate evidence is derived from short isolated scroll runs plus row-level drag isolation checks rather than from a literal one-counter-per-scroll-event probe. Additional runtime harness note to include in the PR: when verifying the scroll gate, record that the interaction window was limited to isolated scroll-only input, and when verifying drag isolation, use the row-level `SortableRow` instrumentation in the same narrow window so the reviewer can interpret the counters correctly.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The execution notes now explicitly cover every audited `VideoEditorProvider` pathway, the keep-vs-remove ref decisions with rationale, the provider-scoped mounted availability boundary that preserves nullable-safe behavior, and the telemetry caveat that M0 render counters are interaction-window based so gate evidence must be described as a bounded harness rather than a literal per-scroll-event probe.
